nameMeanings.name: Cheapman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a merchant or trader, Middle English chap(pe)man, chepman, Old English cēapmann, cēpemann, a compound of cēap ‘barter, bargain, price, property’ + mann ‘man’.
Jewish: adopted probably for a like-sounding or like-meaning name in some other European language; see for example   Kaufman  .

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. John Chapmen (sic) was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Kaufman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Chaim, Emanuel, Moshe, Eytan, Herschel, Zvi, Ari, Aron.
Americanized form of German   Kaufmann  , and a variant of the same Jewish surname.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Kaufman meaning ‘merchant’. Compare Koyfman 2.

variants.name: Chipman
meanings: English: from Middle English chipman (Old English cȳp(e)mann), a side form of chapman, chepman ‘merchant, trader’; see Chapman , with which this name was interchangeable.

variants.name: Copeman
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): patronymic from the early Middle English personal name Cop(e)man, an Anglicized form of Old Scandinavian Kaupmaðr. It is a transferred use of Old Scandinavian kaupmaðr (Old Danish køpman) ‘merchant’, cognate with Old English cēapmann (see Chapman ), but there is no evidence for the occupational term in Middle English.
Americanized form of North German   Koopmann   or Dutch   Koopman  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: John Cheapman was born in 1500, in St Martin by Looe, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. He married F Chipman in 1519, in St Martin by Looe, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1593, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 93, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: John Cheapmanは1500年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルスト マルティン ビー ロオで生まれた。1519年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルスト マルティン ビー ロオでF Chipmanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1593年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで93歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Cheapman(은)는 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 마틴 비 루에서 태어났다. 그는 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 마틴 비 루에서 F Chipman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1593년 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 93세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: John Cheapman родился в 1500 г. в местности ст мартинь би лу, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени F Chipman в 1519 г. в местности ст мартинь би лу, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1593 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 93 лет и был похоронен в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.
